examples: Cherenkov light comes from these particles when the speed of light is exceeded.
The principle of the inverse cherenkov focusing laser accelerator is presented.
Some new work on the Cherenkov counting technique and rapid radiochemical analysis has been published.
Normally, it is impossible to distinguish Cherenkov radiation generated by positrons from that created by electrons.
These create large amounts of Cherenkov radiation, and can be identified because they trigger several widely spaced detectors.
